forum_comments.body: What a rollercoaster of a match. Red City started slowly but really grew into it after the equaliser. Thought the press from the front three was excellent in the second half.
The referee completely lost control in the last 20 minutes. Soft yellow cards all game and then somehow misses that obvious pull in the box on Torres.
Agree. Either book both players or let it go, but the referee only ever seemed to punish Red City for those 50/50 challenges.
Not usually one to blame the referee but tonight was shocking. How did VAR and the on-pitch referee both miss the handball before Blue Uniteds second goal?
Thought Alex Torres was brilliant again. His movement dragged their centre-backs all over the place and he deserved at least one more goal.
People will focus on the referee but Red City also wasted a lot of good chances. On another night this finishes 41.
True, but key moments matter. If the referee gives the pen at 11, its a totally different game and Blue United probably crumble.
From a neutral: entertaining match but inconsistent refereeing for both sides. Some obvious fouls were ignored and then tiny nudges were blown up.
Anyone else think the referee looked nervous from kick-off? First ten minutes he blew for everything, then suddenly let the game get really physical.
Exactly this. If theyre going to use VAR, at least have the referee go to the monitor for big calls like that, not just offside checks.
Lineups are in  surprised to see Torres start again after playing 90 minutes midweek. Hope his legs hold up.
Yeah, but hes in form. If we want to beat Blue United we need our best forward on the pitch from the start.
The problem isnt just VAR, its the overall standard of refereeing. Different referees apply the same rules in completely different ways every weekend.
This. Id rather have a slightly stricter or looser game, as long as the referee is consistent from first minute to last.
Also think communication needs to improve. Fans in the stadium have no idea why VAR is taking so long or what the referee is actually checking.
Id start Torres through the middle with two quick wingers either side. Mountain City hate runners in behind, and it also lets our full-backs bomb on.
For me its between Alex Torres and Marco Silva. Torres is more complete right now, but Silva has ridiculous movement and could explode next season.
Championship Manager 01/02 on an old laptop. Lost entire summers to rebuilding tiny clubs and taking them to the Champions League.
